title: Brickellia microphylla
intro: Brickellia microphylla, also known as small-leaved Brickellia, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Brickellia microphylla include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Brickellia microphylla is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional remedies for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects.
taxonomy: Brickellia microphylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Brickellia, which consists of a group of plants native to North and South America. The plant's classification also places it in the order Asterales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes daisies, sunflowers, and other related species. At the kingdom level, Brickellia microphylla is classified as a Plantae, which encompasses all flowering plants. Its classification at the domain level places it in the Eukaryota, which is a group of complex organisms with cells that have a true nucleus.

benefits: Brickellia microphylla, also known as the Mexican brickellia, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and alleviate pain. Its extracts have been found to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating infections.
